title: Hyderabad Weather on May 4, 2026: Intense Heat Followed by Thunderstorm Relief - NativePlanet
search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Telangana
full_text:
Hyderabad: The weather across Hyderabad on May 4, 2026, is expected to bring a sharp contrast through the day, with extreme daytime heat followed by evening thunderstorms offering partial relief. According to updates aligned with the India Meteorological Department, the city will experience dry, hazy, and hot conditions during the first half of the day, before a shift in weather patterns later.
Temperature and Daytime Conditions
The city is likely to record a maximum temperature of around 41°C, while the minimum may hover near 28°C. Morning hours are expected to remain warm and slightly hazy, with sunlight intensifying rapidly by late morning. By afternoon, conditions will turn harsh and dry, increasing the risk of heat stress.
Wind speeds will stay relatively light, ranging between 4-6 km/h, offering little relief from the heat. Humidity levels are expected to remain moderate to low (around 35-40%), which may reduce stickiness but will make the heat feel sharper and more dehydrating.
Authorities have advised residents to avoid outdoor exposure during peak afternoon hours, stay hydrated, and wear light clothing to minimise heat-related discomfort.
Thunderstorms and Weather Shift
A noticeable change is expected towards the evening and night, when parts of Hyderabad may receive light to moderate rainfall accompanied by thunderstorms and gusty winds. Wind speeds during this period could increase significantly, reaching 30-40 km/h, particularly in open and peripheral areas.
This shift is linked to broader weather activity across Telangana, where similar conditions are forecast in multiple districts. While rainfall in Hyderabad may remain scattered, it is expected to bring temporary cooling and improved air quality after a hot day.
Areas Likely to Be Affected
Within Hyderabad, the impact will be city-wide, though outer zones and open areas may experience stronger winds and more noticeable thunderstorm activity. Key regions that could see weather changes include:
- Secunderabad and central city zones - heat during the day, possible evening clouds
- Gachibowli, HITEC City, Madhapur - dry afternoon heat, followed by light showers
- Kukatpally, Miyapur, Bachupally - gusty winds and scattered rain later in the day
- LB Nagar, Dilsukhnagar, Uppal - evening thunder activity with brief showers
- Shamshabad and outskirts - higher chances of stronger winds and lightning due to open terrain
- State-Wide Outlook
Across Telangana, districts such as Mulugu, Jayashankar Bhupalpally, Warangal, and Hanamkonda are under a yellow alert, with forecasts indicating light to moderate rainfall, lightning, and gusty winds (30-50 km/h).
Final Outlook
May 4 will be a two-phase weather day for Hyderabad, with intense summer heat dominating the daytime and localized thunderstorms bringing evening relief. While no prolonged rainfall is expected, the shift in conditions signals the gradual transition toward more unstable pre-monsoon weather patterns. Residents are advised to remain cautious during both heat and storm phases of the day.

title: Mendora records 46°C as Telangana reels under heat - Hyderabad Mail
full_text:
HYDERABAD : The state continues to reel under intense summer heat, with conditions resembling a heatwave across several regions. Residents are facing high temperatures and severe humidity.
Maximum temperatures above 41°C have been recorded in most parts of the state. Mendora in Nizamabad district recorded the highest temperature of 46°C on Sunday.
The weather department said similar conditions are likely to continue for the next two days and advised people to take precautions. It added that heatwave conditions may prevail in north Telangana districts.
Teen dies of heatstroke in Nalgonda district
In a separate incident, a teenager died of suspected heatstroke in Nalgonda district. The deceased was identified as Matangi Vikram, 16, son of Matangi Madaru and Mutyalamma of Sangaram village.

- Bhadrachalam – 37.5
